title: Presented an open paper session at the 2023 WCCBT
tags: conference, oral presentation, WCCBT, addiction, internet gaming disorder, alcohol use disorder, fMRI, computational modeling
excerpt:
I gave a oral presentation at 2023 WCCBT, titled “Shared and distinct neurocognitive features of internet gaming disorder and alcohol use disorder” 

By collaborating with psychiatric department in Yonsei university, we analyzed task fMRI data fro...
content:
I gave a oral presentation at 2023 WCCBT, titled “Shared and distinct neurocognitive features of internet gaming disorder and alcohol use disorder” 

By collaborating with psychiatric department in Yonsei university, we analyzed task fMRI data from individuals with internet gaming disorder, alcohol use disorder, and healthy control while they perfomred the two-stage Markov decision task. 


It was a significant milestone for me as it was my first time presenting at an international conference. I found the experience to be highly rewarding, as I had the opportunity to showcase my research, engage in discussions about my other projects, and interact with researchers from different locations! I am excited to share that we are in the final stages of completing our work and preparing it for submission!

title: Qualifying exam for PhD
excerpt:
I am delighted to share that I have succesfully passed the PhD thesis qualification test.

I conducted an extensive literature search on my research interest, specifically focusing on addiction. I wrote three comprehensive reviews, each spanning o...
content:
I am delighted to share that I have succesfully passed the PhD thesis qualification test.

I conducted an extensive literature search on my research interest, specifically focusing on addiction. I wrote three comprehensive reviews, each spanning over 30 pages, which explored addiction models, naturalstic fMRI paradigm, and voice data in examining addiction.

Engaging in this literature review allowed me to identify gaps within the existing research and develop innovative ideas for future investigations. I thoroughly enjoyed the process of critically analyzing the literature and contributing to the advancement of knowledge in my field. I am excited to continue building upon this foundation and contribute further to the field of addiction research!

content:
I gave a poster presentation at 2022 SfN, titled “Neuroforecasting exceeds self-reports in predicting cosmetic sales: an fMRI study.” 

Check out the abstract here! 


This was my first time attending SfN in person! 
I thoroughly enjoyed sharing my research, engaging in discussions about my other projects (e.g., naturalistc fMRI paradigm, collecting voice data in studying psychopathology), interacting with researchers, and gaining insights into their work. It was a truly enriching experience.

This...
content:
I gave a poster presentation at 2022 SOBP, titled “Daily Caffeinated Soda Intake in Children is associated With Neurocognitive Vulnerabilities of Substance Misuse and Predicts Greater Alcohol Use in 12 Months.” 

Check out the abstract here! 
This project is based on the ABCD study, and you could see more details in the preprint. 


This was my first time attending an in-person conference abroad! 
I had such a meaningful time introducing my research and interacting with researchers working in various places, and learning about their works!

title: Megazone Tech Talk
excerpt:
After giving a poster presentation at 2021 AI retreat, I was invited to give a talk at Megazone Tech Talk!

I gave a talk on my master theis, titled “Identifying unique neurocognitive mechanisms of food addiction and binge eating using longitudina...
content:
After giving a poster presentation at 2021 AI retreat, I was invited to give a talk at Megazone Tech Talk!

I gave a talk on my master theis, titled “Identifying unique neurocognitive mechanisms of food addiction and binge eating using longitudinal study design and computational modeling.” 

It was a great opportunity to introduce my research and also learn about Megazone. They not only provide cloud services but also offer AI-based consultations to various industries, such as wine recommendation systems or analyzing the impact of Blackpink on YG Entertainment’s building by aggregating data from various social media platforms worldwide. It was quite fascinating!

title: Poster presentation at AI retreat
tags: conference, poster, presentation, AI, food addiction, binge eating, computational modeling, longitudinal study, weight-related decision-making
excerpt:
I gave a poster presentation at the 2021 AI retreat held by the Artificial Intelligence Institute, Seoul National University. 

The conferenece was held in-person, finally! It has been almost 2 years since the last in-person conference. Presenting...
content:
I gave a poster presentation at the 2021 AI retreat held by the Artificial Intelligence Institute, Seoul National University. 

The conferenece was held in-person, finally! It has been almost 2 years since the last in-person conference. Presenting my research to people from other fields, and engaging in discussions with individuals from industries such as Naver Clova, Megazone, Kakao etc., provided me with a great opportunity to broaden my perspective!

title: Poster presentation at SfN 2021
tags: conference, poster, presentation, SfN, food addiction, binge eating, computational modeling, longitudinal study, weight-related decision-making
excerpt: I presented my thesis work, titled “Identifying unique neurocognitive mechanisms of food addiction and binge eating using longitudinal study design and computational modeling” at the 2021 Society for Neuroscience (SfN) Annual Meeting. I had origni...
content:
I presented my thesis work, titled “Identifying unique neurocognitive mechanisms of food addiction and binge eating using longitudinal study design and computational modeling” at the 2021 Society for Neuroscience (SfN) Annual Meeting. I had orignially planned to attend the meeting in person, but it was canceled due to the COVID-19. 
However, I still enjoyed engaging in discussions with our group members and sharing our researche and motivaions through virtual poster session! :)
If you have registered for the 2021 SfN, please check out the poster session! You can view my poster and also 5-minute recordings (Day two, P778 - Modeling and Behavior III #03)

Great thanks to my family!! 
I’m now starting my Ph.D :)
content:
I have successfully received a Master’s degree in Clinical Psychology from Seoul National University :) 
Great thanks to my family!! 
I’m now starting my Ph.D :)

title: Got admission to PhD program
excerpt:
I have been admitted to the Ph.D. program at the Department of Psychology, Seoul National University. 
I am grateful that I could work/study/conduct research in my lab for the next three years and continue my ongoing research projects :) 
I wish a...
content:
I have been admitted to the Ph.D. program at the Department of Psychology, Seoul National University. 
I am grateful that I could work/study/conduct research in my lab for the next three years and continue my ongoing research projects :) 
I wish and believe that my Ph.D. program will provide me with invaluable opportunities for personal and academic growth.

title: Working at the on-campus counseling center
tags: clinical psychology, counseling, practice consultant, clinical experience
excerpt:
Starting this January, I have begun working at the on-campus counseling center at Seoul National University, known as the “Center for Campus Life &amp; Culture”. 
As a graduate student majoring in clinical psychology, this is a fantastic opportuni...
content:
Starting this January, I have begun working at the on-campus counseling center at Seoul National University, known as the “Center for Campus Life &amp; Culture”. 
As a graduate student majoring in clinical psychology, this is a fantastic opportunity for me to gain experience in real clinical settings and apply what I have learned in lectures so far. 
From January to June, I will be conducting intake interviews on a weekly basis. Additionally, I will be conducting 1:1 counseling sessions! 

As a practice counselor, I am eager to learn and grow, and I hope to provide valuable assistance to my future clients!

My presentation titled “Neural Correlates of Goal-Directed Control in Video Game Addiction and Alcohol Use Disorder” can be found there.

title: My work presented at SfN poster session!
tags: conference, poster, presentation, sfN, ABCD, soda, caffeine
excerpt:
My first project using the Adolescent Brain and Cognitive Development (ABCD) study was presented at a poster session in the Society for Neuroscience 2019 meeting (Chicago, IL). The poster was presented by the second author, Hyeonjin Kim. 

The tit...
content:
My first project using the Adolescent Brain and Cognitive Development (ABCD) study was presented at a poster session in the Society for Neuroscience 2019 meeting (Chicago, IL). The poster was presented by the second author, Hyeonjin Kim. 

The title of the poster is “Identifying behavioral and neural predictors of caffeinated soda intake in childhood using a large database from the ABCD study.”

title: Graduated from Seoul National University :)
excerpt:
From 2015 to 2019, I pursued my studies in Psychology and Brain-Mind-Behavior at Seoul National University.  
Now, as a graduate student in the Computational Clinical Science Laboratory, I am excited for the opportunity to conduct remarkable resea...
content:
From 2015 to 2019, I pursued my studies in Psychology and Brain-Mind-Behavior at Seoul National University.  
Now, as a graduate student in the Computational Clinical Science Laboratory, I am excited for the opportunity to conduct remarkable research alongside my  PI, Dr. Woo-Young Ahn, and my fellow colleagues! ;-)

title: Presentation at the Brain-Mind-Behavior symposium
excerpt:
I successfully completed my independent research projects in Fall 2018. 
Using a large database from the ABCD study, my research focused on examining the relationship between children’s caffeinated soda intake and neuro-cognitive deficits. 

Throu...
content:
I successfully completed my independent research projects in Fall 2018. 
Using a large database from the ABCD study, my research focused on examining the relationship between children’s caffeinated soda intake and neuro-cognitive deficits. 

Throughout the process, I gained skills in Python programming, data analysis, poster creation, and presentation presentation.
Additionally, I am thrilled to share that I received a poster award for my work! :-)
